summary: The What's New dialog now scrolls properly so you can read the full list of recent changes.
highlights: BUG FIX: Fixed the What's New dialog so longer change lists scroll instead of getting cut off

summary: The writing assistant gains scope controls, in-place edit refinement, and clearer job status.
highlights: Suggest and Brainstorm now let you choose whether the AI reads just up to your cursor or the whole chapter
You can now refine a proposed edit in place (e.g. ask to make it shorter or colder) instead of regenerating it from scratch
The assistant rail now flags when a tool is working or has just finished on a tab you are not currently viewing
The generating tools now rest as a single Generate button, with the optional steering instruction tucked behind an Add a direction toggle, and idle panels show a centered prompt
A whole-chapter suggestion stays anchored to the block it was generated for, so it inserts in the right place even after you move your cursor
Failed Brainstorm replies no longer leave a truncated partial answer behind, and AI requests that error now report the failure instead of getting stuck loading
The block action toolbar sits a little higher so it no longer covers the first line of a block
Removed the Typographic/Cards block style setting in favor of a single consistent layout

summary: Find and replace comes to the chapter editor.
highlights: Press Cmd+F in a chapter to open a find and replace bar that searches across all blocks
Match the current result is highlighted in place and the view scrolls to center it as you step through results
Toggle match case, whole word, and regular expression search, with a live count of how many matches were found
Jump between matches with the up/down arrows or Enter and Shift+Enter, wrapping around at the ends
Replace the current match or replace all at once, with capture group support like $1 and $2 in regex mode

summary: Smarter AI panel scoping, full layout restore on relaunch, and a fixed UI freeze
highlights: Critique and Continuity now let you choose what the AI reads: up to your cursor or the whole chapter
BUG FIX: The app reopens exactly as you left it, restoring window size and position, the PDF and Outline panels, and your last-open chapter
BUG FIX: Fixed a freeze (beach ball) that could lock up the app while using the Claude or Codex CLI providers
The Edit tab now disables its prompt with clearer guidance when no editable block is selected
Removed the AI Cast tab; character tracking now lives in outline cast assignments

summary: Lore entries get full worldbuilding details and your window now reopens where you left it
highlights: Lore entries now hold a description, tagged labels, and linked characters, all editable in a new slide-over panel opened from the sidebar or any outline card
Assign lore to outline cards, where it shows as a tinted chip alongside characters, and the AI sculpt feature now reads lore descriptions for better suggestions
The character color picker adds a 27-shade preset grid plus a custom color option using the native OS color panel
The app window remembers its size and position and reopens exactly where you last closed it
Typing in the editor no longer jolts the page upward when you are working at the bottom of a chapter

summary: Quickly copy the location of your compiled PDF straight from the preview pane.
highlights: A new copy button next to preview.pdf in the PDF preview pane copies the full file path of the compiled PDF to your clipboard, with a checkmark confirming the copy
Hovering the button shows the exact PDF path, so you can grab the file's location without leaving the app

summary: Launching the installed app now finds your installed tools so PDF export and other features work
highlights: Fixed PDF/LaTeX compilation failing when the app was opened normally from Finder, the Dock, or the .dmg instead of a terminal
The app now reliably locates your installed tools (LaTeX, git, and the AI CLIs) on macOS and Linux no matter where they were installed

summary: A visual story outline storyboard, writing stats and streaks, automatic app updates, and multi-block AI editing
highlights: New Outline storyboard: plan your novel on a chapter-column board grouped into act bands, with a logline, per-chapter spine (premise, goal, conflict, turn), cast assignments, and drag-and-drop plot-element cards.
AI Sculpt reshapes a chapter's plot elements with proposed add/rewrite/move/remove changes you review and keep or skip before anything is applied.
AI suggestions are now grounded in your outline, so continuations, edits, and critiques aim at the chapter's intended structure.
Open the Outline from the sidebar, the top bar, the command palette, or Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O, with a read-only chapter plan mirrored in the right panel.
New Stats tab shows total words written, days written, current and longest writing streaks, and an activity heatmap.
Multi-block selection: Cmd/Ctrl-click to select several blocks and run the AI Edit tool across all of them at once.
Insert a new block above or below any block from its context or actions menu.
Automatic updates: the app checks for new versions on launch, offers a download with a See changes link, and adds a What's New changelog dialog plus a macOS Check for Updates menu item.
Use your Codex CLI or Claude Code subscription as the AI provider instead of an OpenAI API key, selectable in Settings.
Redesigned Settings as a tabbed dialog covering Appearance, AI, Backup, Keyboard, Stats, and About.
The right assistant panel is now resizable and remembers its width, with a clearer toggle button in the top bar.
The backup indicator now tracks your edits live, updating as you write instead of only on a manual check.
Reopening a project now shows the existing PDF as already built instead of reporting it as not built.
Typography refresh across dialogs, panels, and the editor for a cleaner, more consistent look.

summary: The first tracked release of aproprose.
highlights: Managed novel projects: create a new novel, then add, rename, reorder, and delete chapters from the sidebar.
Rich-text editing with bold and italic from the selection toolbar or Cmd/Ctrl+B and Cmd/Ctrl+I, plus centered scene-break headings.
Split and carve blocks directly from a text selection.
AI panel with brainstorm chat, click-to-generate drafting, and per-block edit suggestions you can review and accept or reject.
Pick your OpenAI model in Settings; the assistant stays gated until a key and model are set.
Git and GitHub backup and sync with a full-screen diff review.
Command palette (Cmd/Ctrl+K) and registry-driven keyboard shortcuts with on-screen hints.
Build-error viewer that surfaces LaTeX compile failures with a one-click View action.
Custom frameless window with a platform-adaptive titlebar, plus light, sepia, and dark themes.
